def: s. f. Smallness.
s. m. (dakh.) Littleness, minority; childhood.
n. f. The sound made by washermen beating clothes against a stone. Baṛī chhuāchhū ho rahī hai .

n. f. The refuse left after pressing the juice of sugar-cane, etc. chhūchhā, chhūchhī , adj. 1 (Tir. chhuchh ) Only; sole; pure. Bāssā tau chhūchā ghī pīvaë saë . Rus. The king lives on butter. (The rustic's idea of supreme felicity). 2. Empty ( khālī ); hollow. Bharlī hāṅdī bharī pūrī, Chhūṅchhī hāṅdī bāje ṭan ṭan . Bhoj. Prov. A vessel full gives forth no sound, The empty vessel doth resound. 3. Contemptible; despicable; shallow. chhūch uṛānā , v. a. See bhus uṛānā , 1.
s. (dakh.) A sort of squib: see چهچهوندر chhuchhūndar, sign. 2.
n. m. Blame ( ilzām ); calumny.
v. a. To whitewash.
s. m. A date. (Phœnix dactylifera).
s. f. Touch.
s. f. Chalk.
n. f. S. छुपन्मृता. 1. The sensitive plant; mimosa pudica . 2. Any thing very delicate or frail. 3. A touchy, testy, sensitive, or irritable person.
n. f. Babble; murmur.

v. a. 1. To make a rippling sound; babble; murmur. 2. To come off (as loose fœces ) with a rumbling interrupted noise. 3. To make water slowly (as a mare, etc. in heat). chhul chhul karte phir'nā , v. n. To flit about restlessly, now here, now there.
adj. Boyish, puerile.
n. f. H. chhoṭā little, unglī finger. The little finger.
s. f. Liberation.
n. f. Ransom.
adj. chhū touch. Defiled by touching; polluted (applied to drinking vessels, dishes, etc.); impure; unclean.
more: adj. Defiled by touching, foul, corrupt, polluted (applied to vessels, dishes, &c.).

def: v. a. To cause to be separated. See چهرَانا Dūdh chhuṭā diyā . The child has been weaned.
adj. (dakh.) Being let go, being shot forth.
n. f. Remission of revenue.

n. m. H. chhuṭnā to be freed. 1. Liberation; disengagement; release; discharge; acquittal; deliverance; liberty; exemption. 2. Leisure; spare time; time. Ghar se zarā chhuṭkārā nahīṅ hotā. chhuṭkārā pānā yā h. , v. n. To be freed, released; to get rid of. chhuṭkārā denā , v. a. To set free; let off; release; acquit; discharge from liability.
v. n. See chhuṭnā. Tum rūṭhe, ham chhūṭe . Prov. You are angry, I am free.
intj. Fy! foh! faugh! — ćhī-ćhī, s.f. Dirt, filth, excrement; — (colloq.) an epithet applied to a class of Eurasians (by whom the expression ćhī is commonly used); — intj. Fy! shame! &c. (see ćhī).
n. m. Pudendum muliebre .
adj. (f. -ī), Shallow, of little depth.
s.f. Shallowness.
s.f. The play of ducks and drakes
more: s. f. The play of ducks and drakes. چهچهلي کهيلنا chhichhlī khelnā, To play at ducks and drakes.
s. f. The play of a dog or cat with game, &c.
subs: To play at ducks and drakes.

ety: Prk. छुच्छअडओ (with w inserted); S. तुच्छक+र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Light, vain, airy, petty, trifling, frivolous, puerile, childish, shallow, superficial; tawdry, showy, ostentatious; low, mean, insignificant, contemptible.
adj. = ćhićhorā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Light, vain, airy, petty, trifling, frivolous, puerile, childish, shallow, superficial; tawdry, showy, ostentatious; low, mean, insignificant, contemptible.
s.m. Lightness, airiness, frivolity, puerility; shallowness; meanness; show, display, &c.
s.m. = ćhićṛā, q.v. s.m. Skin, cuticle, integument, the cellular membrane that covers the surface of the body; slough (of a wound), crust (of a sore); the prepuce, foreskin; entrails; offal.
s.m. Skin, membrane, &c. ( = ćhićṛā, q.v.); the skinny part of meat, tough and uneatable part of flesh
more: s. m. The skinny and rejected part of meat, a piece of flesh.
subs: To flay; to tear to pieces.

def: v. n. To scrawl, to score.
adj. (f. -ī), ćhićhorā, q.v.
more: adj. H. chhī pooh, chhorā boy. 1. Childish; shallow; light; trifling; mean; base. 2. Showy; ostentatious.

def: s.m. Skin, cuticle, integument, the cellular membrane that covers the surface of the body; slough (of a wound), crust (of a sore); the prepuce, foreskin; entrails; offal.
more: s. m. Sink, slough (of a wound), skin.

def: adj. Skinny, lean.
adj. Thin, not close (as cloth, &c.), sparse, straggling, widely separated, &c. ( = ćhidrā, q.v.).
more: adj. Thin; not close. See chhidrā .
adj. Thin, not close (said of a person or animal whose legs are much separated).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Pierced, bored, perforated.
v.t. To cause to pierce; to perforate; to get pierced or perforated ( = ćhidwānā).
more: v. a. Caus. of چهيدنا q. v.
(causal of چهيدنا chhednā ) v. a. To cause to pierce, to perforate.

def: v.n. To be pierced, to be bored
adj. (f. -ī), Containing gaps or openings, thin (as cloth, a crop, &c.), not closely woven; sparse, meagre; straggling, widely separated, wide apart (as the legs of a person or animal; = ćhīdā).
more: adj. Opp. of ghankā . Thin (cloth, crop); spare; meagre; not close; straggling. Kaisā chhidrā goṭā bunā hai .

def: s.m. A hole, orifice, opening, aperture, a crack; a defect, fault, flaw; weakness, weak point; — adj. Pierced, perforated (see ćhidrā; chīdā).
more: n. m. S. छिद्र A hole ( chhed ).

def: v.t. To place at wide intervals, to spread out; — v.n. To walk with the legs far apart; — ćhidrā-kar ćalnā, v.n. Idem.
more: v. a. To spread out; to place at wide intervals. chhidrā-kar chalnā, chhidrānā , v. n. To walk with one's legs spread out, or with difficulty.

def: s.f. = S چهدرتو छिद्रत्व ćhidratva, s.m. The state of being perforated, the quality of giving space, holes, or openings; the quality of being pervaded by everything.
s.m. = S چهدرتا छिद्रता ćhidratā, s.f. The state of being perforated, the quality of giving space, holes, or openings; the quality of being pervaded by everything.
part. Pierced, perforated, having holes, gaps, or openings.
v.t. To cause to be pierced, to havo or get pierced or bored.
s. Name of a city of Turkistan, the people of which are extremely handsome, and unequalled in archery: clay, mud.
v. a. To embellish, to decorate.
adj. Seventy-six.
s. m. 1. Mud, ooze, slime, mire. 2. A splinter of wood. چـﮩ‍لا نکلنا chihlā nikalnā, To fatigue, to be tired. چـﮩ‍لا کرنا chihlā-karnā, To split to pieces.
more: s. m. A large wooden peg, a tent-pin.

def: (from چـﮩ‍لا chihlā ) adj. Splashy, muddy, slimy.
s. m. See چـنـﮩـ chinh.
(or, dakh. chahr ) s. Face: see چـﮩره chihra.
s.f. Loss, waste, wane, diminution, ebb (of tide, &c.); destruction, decay, wasting or wearing away; — ćhīj-baṭṭā, ćhīj-ćhapaṭ, s.m. Tare and tret.
more: n. f. S. क्षय from क्षि to wane. 1. Decrease; diminution; wane; ebb; decay. 2. Waste; wear and tear; loss; damage. Man meṅ ser kī chhīj hai. chhīj baṭṭā, chīj chhapaṭ , n. m. Tare and tret.

def: v.t. To cause to waste or wear away; to decrease, to lessen.

ety: ćhīj˚ = Prk. छिज्ज(इ) = S. छिद्य(ते), pass. fr. rt. छिद्
def: v.n. To decrease, dwindle, diminish, wane; to decay, waste away, fade, wither, pine away, become emaciated, to wear away (by attrition, &c.), to be effaced or removed
more: v. n. 1. To decrease ( ghaṭnā ); dwindle; wane; decay. Banaj kare to ṭoṭā āë, baiṭh khāë dhan chhīje, Kaheṅ Kabīr, suno bhai sādhoṅ, māṅg khāë so jīte . Kabīr. Who trade must suffer loss, who sit at home their wealth must drain, Kabīr hear, brother saint! who begs and eats his is the gain. 2. To shrivel; lose flesh; waste; pine away. 3. To be effaced or removed (evil); to be affected by; to receive impressions. Pānī meṅ pākhān, bhīje, par chhīje nahīṅ; Mūrakh āge gyān, rījhe, par būjhe nahīṅ . Prov. As stone in water's wet, but no impression takes, So laughs the fool at words, their meaning he mistakes. 4. To die; expire ( utarnā , 32). Bachcha chhīj gayā .

v. n. To decrease, to pine away. ديکها ديکهي کيجي جوگ چهيجي کايا باڙهي روگ dekhā dekhī kīje jog, chhīje kāyā bāṛhe rog, They who merely imitate others in austerity, waste away, and their diseases increase.

def: s.m. = ćhīṅkā, q.v. s.m. A network of strings or cords to hang anything in (as food, pots, a burthen at either end of a yoke or pole, &c.); the cords of a bahangī, q.v.; a rope-bridge across a hill-torrent, or a rope with a sling or basket attached (by which travellers are drawn across); — a muzzle (for cattle).
more: s. m. A sling for carrying baskets, &c. See چهينکا chhīnkā.

def: n. m. S. चिक्कणत्व greasiness. The dirt which settles on a lamp. chīkaṭ, chikkaṭ , adj. Oily; greasy; dirty; soiled.
s. (dakh.) Clamminess ( Harris ).
v. n. S. चिक्कण To be matted, sticky, dirty, filthy, etc. Sir chikaṭ rahā hai .
s. f. See چيخ chīḵẖ.
Oudh, n. f. Wages for weeding ( nalāī ).
s.m. Sneezing (see ćhīṅknā).
verbal noun (dakh. see چکلنا chikalnā ) Chewing.
v.n. To be cut or crossed out, to be cancelled; to be detained, be stopped, be arrested, &c.; to be confiscated, be sequestrated.
n. f. 1. ( chiknāī ) Grease; butter; oil; fat. 2. Glossiness; glaze; shine; sleekness. 3. Smoothness; polish.
s. f. 1. Fat of meat. 2. Wantonness. 3. Gloss, smoothness, polish.
n. f. 1. A clayey soil. 2. Rich, highly cultivated land.
s. f. (dakh.) Potterʼs earth, clay.
s.f. A switch, a stick, a wand ( = ćhaṛī).
s.f. Parings, scrapings (of rind or peel), clippings, shavings, sortings.
more: n. f. S. छल्लि bark Parings ( chhipṭī ).

def: v.t. = ćhilwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be skinned, or peeled, &c.; to have (a thing) peeled, pared, or scraped, &c.
more: (caus. of چهونا) v. a. To cause to touch.

def: s. f. (dakh.) Scream, shriek, screech ( Harris ).

ety: ćhilkā or ćhāl+aurī = Prk. उल्लिआ = S. ऊल+इका
def: s.f. A blister ( = ćhālā); a whitlow, a felon.
more: s. f. A whitlow, a blister, a felon.

def: n. m. A small slender nut; the seed of the edible pine.
s. f. See چيل chīl.
n. f. Body-lice (Sant. si ).
v. a. To peck.
(dakh.) A game amongst children, not unlike our "rules of contrary" ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.m. Skin, cuticle, epidermis; bark, rind; shell, peel, husk, hull; crust, scale
more: n. m. S. छल्लि skin. Rind; peel; skin; husk; crust; shell; scales. chhilkā utārnā , v. a. To peel; husk; shell; blanch.
s. m. Crust, husk, shell, peel, scale, rind, bark, skin. چهلکا اتارنا chhilkā utārnā, To blanch, peel, husk, decorticate.
subs: (-kā), To blanch, peel, husk, shell, decorticate.

def: s.m. = ćhilkā.
n. f. Cry; outcry; screams; hue and cry.
s.m. The husk of ćanā, q.v.
more: s. m. The husk of chanā, q. v.

ety: prob. akin to ćholṅā, q.v.; or fr. छिद्र
def: v.t. To skin, excoriate, peel, pare, bark, to scratch, scrape, erase; — (fig.) to ask the same thing again and again; to make a nice point of
more: v. a. 1. See chhilṅā , v. n. Bachan sās yoṅ chholat sai jī; bāḍhī chhol kare jaise . Wom. Rus. My mother-in-law's words cut me to the quick, just as a carpenter takes off chips. 2. To ask the same thing again and again; to make a nice point of.
v. a. To peel, skin, excoriate, pare, bark (a tree, &c.), erase, scrape. چهيلنا چهالنا chhīlnā-chhālnā or چهيل چهال کرنا chhīl chhāl karnā, v. a. To pare, to peel, &c.

def: v.n. To be excoriated; to be peeled, be shelled, be husked; to be erased, be scraped, be scratched out (see ćhīlnā).
n. m. A fried pulse meal-cake.
s.f. Peeling, paring, husking, &c.; — price paid for peeling or husking, &c.
v.t. To cause to be skinned, or peeled, &c.; to have (a thing) peeled, pared, or scraped, &c.
more: Caus. of چهيلنا chhilvā'ī , n. f. 1. Peeling; husking, etc. 2. Price paid for peeling, etc.
(caus. of چهيلنا) v. a. To cause to skin or excoriate, &c.

def: v. n. To cleave to.

v. n. 1. To cling to ( chipaknā , 1). 2. To be pasted, gummed, glued (Sant. lāṭhā ). 3. To persecute ( pīchhe paṛnā ). 4. To have sexual intercourse with; make an indecent assault. 5. To clasp; embrace; hug; to stick to like a burr. Bhūt to nahīṅ chimaṭ gayā? 6. To charge falsely.

leather (winged) bird; chamgīdṛī ; Old H. chamgadal , n. f. H. chām leather, gīdaṛ a jackal; hence, a leather (winged) fox. A bat ( bādur ); flying fox. Chamgiddaṛoṅ ke ghar mehmān āë, 'ham bhī laṭkeṅ, tum bhī laṭko .' Prov. A guest did of a bat claim hospitality, Says he, 'for a jolly treat let's both hang from a tree.'
s.f. A pod, legume; a bean; green pease, &c. in the pod (cf. maṭar).
more: Tir. n. f. S. शिम्बी A pod or legume; bean ( phalī ).
s. f. A pod, legume, husk.

def: s. f. Power, rigour, flexibility, ductility.
adj. Infrangible (a quill, for instance), hard.

Mah. n. m. Dim. chimtī , n. f. Tongs; forceps; pincers; nippers, used chiefly for taking up fire. 1. Hāth na jalāiyo, chimṭe-vālā ā gayā hai . Delhi street cry. Dont burn your fingers, I've brought you my pincers! 2. Ek purakh kā kālā raṅg, Gol sīs, do lambī ṭaṅg, Nār ko apnī let uṭhāë, Piyār kare aur chimṭā jāë . Riddle. Ans. Chimṭā tongs. A black complexioned male, round head and long legs hail, His female lifts on high and squeezes her, oh fie!
v. a. Caus. of چمتَا q. v.
v. a. To pinch.
adj. Wasted, worn away; emaciated, thin, feeble; delicate, slender, slight, little; poor, miserable.
more: adj. S. क्षय destruction. Thin; emaciated; wasted ( patlā , 3.).
adj. Wasted, emaciated, thin, slender, slight, little.

def: n. f. S. ची to collect. A wrinkle; gather. chīṅ ba-jabīṅ h. , v. n. To knit the brows; frown. chīn-i-peshānī , Ped. A frown; scowl.
more: n. f. S. चित scream. The chirp or call of young birds. chīṅ bulānā , v. a. To defeat; beat ( harānā ). chīṅ bol'nā, yā mānnā , v. n. 1. To cry out; squeal. 2. To be defeated, beaten ( hārnā ). 3. To acknowledge oneself beaten; to give in. chīṅ paṛākh, chīṅ chapaṭ . Sauce; impudence; cheek. chīṅ chīṅ yā cheṅ cheṅ k. , v. n. To chirp; squeal; chatter. Abe! kyoṅ chīṅ chīṅ kartā hai?

def: s.m. Snatching, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: ćhīnā-jhapṭī, s.f. Snatching, taking by force
ćhīnā-ćhīnī, s.f. Mutual snatching and tugging; pulling backwards and forwards; scrambling, scramble (syn. kashmakash)

ety: ćhī˚ = Prk. छिव(इ) or छिह(इ) = S. स्पृश(ति), rt. स्पृश्; cf. ćhīhnā
def: v.t. (dialec.) = ćhūnā, q.v. s.m. Touching, touch, contact.
v.t. (dialec.), To sting, to bite (cf. ćheʼonā).
s.m. Snatching, &c. (used in comp.)
more: v. n. 1. To be torn, slit, rent (Sant. oṛe ), worn out. 1. Silāī chhīnā . 2. Bāliyoṅ ke bojh se kān chhī gaë . 2. To turn as milk.
n. m. A sweetmeat made of curds.
s. m. 1. Curd. 2. A small kind of cymbal.
v. a. (dakh.) 1. To bite, sting: see کاٿنا kāṭnā and ڐسنا ḍasnā. 2. To touch, &c. See چهونا chhūnā.
v. a. To pull, tear, pluck, rob, snatch. چهينا چهاني کرنا chhīnā-chhānī karnā, To scramble. چهين لينا chhīn lenā, To snatch up, acquire, take possession of, seize, pluck, to bereave, deprive.
subs: ćhīnā-jhapṭī, s.f. Snatching, taking by force
ćhīnā-ćhīnī, s.f. Mutual snatching and tugging; pulling backwards and forwards; scrambling, scramble (syn. kashmakash)

def: s.m. & adv., contrac. fr. ćhin-ek, q.v.s.v. ćhin.
v.t. To winnow, to sift.
v.n. To blow the nose; — to go away offended.
s. (dakh.) A sort of shoe or slipper without any back piece, the quarters terminating under the ankles on each side, with raised heels, perhaps an inch high ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.f. A harlot, a prostitute, a wanton, a drab, a trull
more: n. f. H. chhī fie, nār woman. A loose woman. 1. Ochhī pīt chhināl kī nibhe na /?/kāhū sāth, Nāu kī sī ārsī har kāhū ke hāth . Dohā. The shallow love of harlot ne'er kept faith with any yet, Like barber's shaving glass, now in this hand, now in that set. 2. Chāl chale lach-ke sagrī, ālī, ghūṅgaṭ meṅ kachhū daras dikhāve, Bāt kare haṅs-ke muskānī, bhaveṅ maṛoṛ-ke nain laṛāve, Āge ko pāoṅ dhare jab sundar, ghūṅgat ke mis chhātī dikhāve, Etī to bāt janāë chalī, ālī, aur chhināl kyā ḍhol bajāve? Kabit. Her frame with flexive motions quivering from her zone, Between the edges of her veil her face just shown, Smiling and laughing while she talks, brows knit, eyes strive, Her veil now drawn her breast to show she doth contrive, Such hints she gives, to beat a drum sure need was none. chhināl-dīdah , adj. Wanton-eyed. ghuṅghṭī chhināl , n. f. A prude; a sly coquette.
s. f. A harlot, a prostitute. چهنال پن يا پنا chhināl-pan or -panā, s. m. Harlotry, prostitution, whoredom.

def: s.m. A dissolute character, a profligate, a fornicator, a rake.
s.f. Harlotry, prostitution; fornication, adultery
more: n. m. Adultery; fornication. 1. Maiṅ ne kuchh chorī, chhinālā to nahīṅ kīyā . Wom. 2. Bin kuṭnāpe chhinālā nahīṅ . Prov. No adultress without a procuress. 3. Mā beṭī meṅ chhinālā nahīṅ chhuptā . Prov. The liaisons of mother and daughter cannot be hid from each other.
s. m. Fornication. چهنالاکرنا chhinālā karnā, To whore, commit adultery.

ety: the preceding, with S. इका for कः

s.m. Seizing, seizure.
s.f. = ćhināl, q.v. s.f. A harlot, a prostitute, a wanton, a drab, a trull
s.m. = ćhinālā, q.v. s.f. Harlotry, prostitution; fornication, adultery
v.t. To water; to throw out water.
more: v. a. To throw out water.
v. a. To mince, to hack, to pound.

def: s.m. & adv. See s.v. ćhin. adj. Wasted, worn away; emaciated, thin, feeble; delicate, slender, slight, little; poor, miserable.
n. f. S. चित्कार. The trumpeting of an elephant.
v. n. To scream; roar.
n. m. H. chīṅ cry, S. पोत the young of an animal. 1. The unfledged young of birds. 2. A family of children.
s.f. The little finger.
s. m. Mark, spot, stain, scar, sign, symbol, token by which any thing is known, feature, signature. چنـﮩـ چڙهانا chinh chaṛhānā, To stigmatize.

n. f. S. चिन्ह Pr. chendham, chindham . 1. A mark; trace; line; track; sign; symptom; token; type; criterion. 2. Lineaments; features ( khat o khāl ) 3. A badge; symbol; emblem. chinhār' , E. Rus. n. m. Com. jān pahchhān . An acquaintance. chinhār'ī , n. f. Acquaintance. chin'hit , adj. Marked; known; recognized.
s. m. An acquaintance.
s. f. Acquaintance.

ety: S. छिक्का; or verb. n. fr. ćhīṅknā
def: s.f. Sneezing; a sneeze
more: n. f. S. छिक्का Sneezing; sneeze. 1. Chhīṅk huī hai, zarā ṭhair jāo! Wom. Somebody sneezed, wait a little! (It is considered unlucky to go anywhere when some one has sneezed). 2. Chhīṅk na ho khaṛe ho jāo . Hin. Wom. Get up quick before any one sneezes.
(from چهينکنا) s. f. Sequestration of goods. چهينک لينا chhenk lenā, v. a. To confiscate.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀙𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸 (chikkā), from Sanskrit छिक्का (chikkā, onomatopoeia).

def: s.m. A network of strings or cords to hang anything in (as food, pots, a burthen at either end of a yoke or pole, &c.); the cords of a bahangī, q.v.; a rope-bridge across a hill-torrent, or a rope with a sling or basket attached (by which travellers are drawn across); — a muzzle (for cattle).
more: n. m. S. शिक्य 1. A net in which pots, etc. are hung as in a swinging tray. Ch/?/īṅke kī pe rakk'iī milegī . Prov. The sneeze foretells my bread I'll get all ready placed in the chhīakā (Said by one who has no faith in the superstition that a sneeze is a bad omen). 2. (For the smallest offence your head is cut off). 3. Kyā maiṅ sej pe chhīṅkat āī, Jo adhbar khel sej ṭū/?/ī; Kyoṅ bai hat āj sej ṭūṭī? Wom. song. Did I come hapless sneezing to my bed That it should break with pleasure but half fed? Scarce sat upon to-day why broke this bed?

ety: ćhīn˚ = Prk. छिन्न(इ) or छिन्ने(इ), fr. S. छिन्न p.p.p. of rt. छिद्

( chheṛ chhāṛ ) Jokes; jests; (Sant. jotet ) chaff; badinage. 4. ( chheṛ-khānī, chher-chhāṛ ) Flirtation; lewd conversation or jokes. 1. Chheṛ khūbāṅ se chalī jāë, Asad, Gar nahīṅ vasl to hasrat hī sahī . Gālib. Grant, Asad, but the laugh and jest with maidens fair prevail, Though fate deny us union close this much contented hail! chheṛ chhāṛ , n. f. 1. See chheṛ , (1, 3, 4.). 2. Now arms around the loved one's neck, now elbows press, now pinches deal, Anon the lips in kisses join, or honeyed words of love reveal. 2. A handful. Bhejyo, māī, chuṭkī! Mendicants' cry. A portion of grain, about two seers, the weighman's fee. chuṭkī bajānā , v. a. 1. To snap the fingers. 2. To fillip with the fingers as an accompaniment to a song. chuṭkī lagānā , v. a. 1.
